description_long: This object is a head of Romanesco broccoli, notable for its striking fractal geometry and vibrant yellow-green color. The surface is covered with tightly packed, conical florets arranged in a spiral pattern, each floret displaying a miniature version of the overall structure. The crown is dense and pyramidal, with the largest florets at the base and progressively smaller ones toward the tip. The base of the broccoli reveals a short, thick stalk with a few attached green leaves curling outward. The texture appears bumpy and rough due to the multitude of pointed florets, while the leaves at the base are smoother and more flexible. The overall appearance is fresh, with no visible blemishes or discoloration.
description: A vibrant, fractal-patterned Romanesco broccoli with tightly packed, conical florets and a few green leaves at the base. The crown is dense and pyramidal, displaying a striking yellow-green color.
description_view_0: The broccoli is seen from above, highlighting the dense, spiraling arrangement of pointed florets and a few leaves peeking from the lower edge.
description_view_1: This angled side view emphasizes the three-dimensional, pyramidal structure and reveals more of the attached green leaves at the base.
description_view_2: A direct front view shows the symmetrical, triangular crown and the compact clustering of florets, with the stalk and leaves partially visible at the bottom.
description_view_3: The opposite side view displays the fractal floret pattern and the overall bulk of the crown, with fewer leaves visible compared to other views.
attribute_floret_clustering: Florets are densely packed in a tight, spiral arrangement with minimal gaps; floret size decreases from base to tip, maintaining consistent spacing.
attribute_stalk_segmentation: Stalk is mostly continuous with a single prominent base segment; segmentation is not clearly visible, and nodes are subtle.
attribute_branching_pattern: Branching is highly regular and symmetrical, with primary branches forming a pyramidal structure and secondary branches creating repeating fractal patterns.
attribute_leaf_attachment: A few green leaves are attached at the base of the stalk, clustered and oriented outward; no leaves are distributed along the stalk or crown.
attribute_crown_density: The crown is solid and highly dense, with no visible gaps or open areas between the tightly clustered florets.
